import numpy as np

def update_edge_distance_v2(edge_distance: np.ndarray, local_opt_tour: np.ndarray, edge_n_used: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Args:
        edge_distance (np.ndarray): Original edge distance matrix.
        local_opt_tour (np.ndarray): Local optimal solution path.
        edge_n_used (np.ndarray): Matrix representing the number of times each edge is used.
    Return:
        updated_edge_distance: updated score of each edge distance matrix.
    """
    num_nodes = edge_distance.shape[0]
    updated_edge_distance = np.copy(edge_distance)

    # Calculate the total number of edges in the tour
    total_edges = num_nodes

    # Calculate the average usage of all edges in the tour
    avg_usage = np.mean(edge_n_used[local_opt_tour[:-1], local_opt_tour[1:]])
    avg_usage += edge_n_used[local_opt_tour[-1], local_opt_tour[0]] / total_edges

    # Calculate the standard deviation of the edge usage
    edge_usages = np.concatenate([edge_n_used[local_opt_tour[:-1], local_opt_tour[1:]].flatten(),
                                 [edge_n_used[local_opt_tour[-1], local_opt_tour[0]]]])
    std_usage = np.std(edge_usages)

    # Update the edge distances based on the average and standard deviation of the edge usage
    for i in range(num_nodes - 1):
        current_node = local_opt_tour[i]
        next_node = local_opt_tour[i + 1]

        # Calculate the normalized usage of the edge
        normalized_usage = (edge_n_used[current_node, next_node] - avg_usage) / (std_usage + 1e-10)

        # Update the edge distance based on the normalized usage
        updated_edge_distance[current_node, next_node] *= (1 + np.tanh(normalized_usage))

    # Update the last edge in the tour
    last_node = local_opt_tour[-1]
    first_node = local_opt_tour[0]
    normalized_usage = (edge_n_used[last_node, first_node] - avg_usage) / (std_usage + 1e-10)
    updated_edge_distance[last_node, first_node] *= (1 + np.tanh(normalized_usage))

    return updated_edge_distance
